title: Slow-cooker beef bourguignon
subtitle: Braised beef, lardons, mushrooms, and red wine
excerpt: Beef chuck braised low and slow with red wine, lardons, and chestnut mushrooms until the sauce is glossy and the meat falls apart. The slow cooker handles the long braise without attention. Brown the beef and fry the lardons first for the right depth of flavour.
sourceNotes: Boeuf bourguignon is a Burgundian braise dating to at least the 19th century as a method for tenderising tough working cattle. The classic recipe in Julia Child's Mastering the Art of French Cooking (1961) became the standard English-language reference. The slow cooker adaptation follows the same principle: a full bottle of red wine, lardons, mushrooms, and enough time for the collagen in chuck to dissolve into the sauce.

content.text: Do not skip the browning step here. Beef bourguignon depends on the fond, the dark caramelised residue left in the pan after browning the beef and frying the lardons. Deglaze it with the wine before adding it to the slow cooker. Without this step the result is stewed rather than braised.

items.name: Red wine
items.prepNote: good Burgundy or Côtes du Rhône

content.text: Fry the {{lardons}} in a wide pan over medium heat until the fat renders and the edges crisp. Remove with a slotted spoon and add to the slow cooker. Increase the heat to high. Pat the {{beef-chuck}} dry and season with {{sea-salt-fine}} and {{black-pepper}}. Brown in batches in the same pan, 2 to 3 minutes per side without moving. Transfer each batch to the slow cooker. Add the {{onion}} to the pan and fry for 3 minutes. Add the {{garlic}} and {{tomato-puree}} and cook for 1 minute. Pour in the {{red-wine}} and scrape up all the browned bits from the bottom. Add the {{stock-beef}} and bring to a simmer. Pour over the beef in the slow cooker. Tuck in the {{thyme-fresh}} and {{bay-leaves}}. Cook on low for 8 hours.

In the last 30 minutes, fry the {{mushrooms-chestnut}} in {{unsalted-butter}} over high heat until browned. Stir them into the slow cooker. Remove the {{thyme-fresh}} sprigs and {{bay-leaves}} before serving. If the sauce needs thickening, ladle some into a small saucepan, reduce over high heat for 5 minutes, and stir back in.

items.fix: Always bring the wine to a boil and deglaze the pan before adding it. The long, slow cook mellows the wine, but raw wine added directly stays sharp
items.cause: Red wine not reduced before going into the slow cooker
items.symptom: Sauce thin and wine-tasting rather than rounded

items.fix: Chuck is the right cut. If it has gone dry it has slightly overcooked, reduce the time on the next attempt to 6 to 7 hours
items.cause: Chuck overcooked past the collapse point, or cut was too lean
items.symptom: Beef dry and stringy rather than yielding

items.fix: Always fry mushrooms at high heat before adding them. Raw mushrooms release too much water in the slow cooker
items.cause: Added raw to the slow cooker rather than browned separately

content.text: Beef bourguignon is a weekend dish, the prep time alone means it is not a weeknight option, but the slow cooker removes the need to watch it on the stove for 3 hours. Start it before midday and it is ready for dinner. Serve with mashed potato or crusty bread to absorb the sauce, which is the best part of the dish.
